issues.blurb: The cab-over bunk seams and front cap take road spray and weather, and failing sealant there is a classic source of leaks and soft floors.
The cutaway chassis (often Ford E-series/Transit or Chevy) sees brake, suspension, and drivetrain wear from carrying the heavy house structure.
Slide-out rooms develop motor, gear, and seal problems that cause sticking, water intrusion, or uneven travel.
The water pump, converter, fridge, and propane appliances fail or act up, disrupting onboard living systems.
Rear chassis tires often age out before the tread wears, and aged sidewalls risk blowouts on the highway.
Roof and vent sealant cracks with sun and age, allowing water past the membrane and into the ceiling if not maintained.

narrative.headline: What Class C motorhome owners report most
narrative.summary: These are issues commonly reported by Class C owners as a general reference, not expert-verified data and possibly outdated. The cab-over bunk leaks, cutaway chassis wear, and slide-outs are what owners tend to mention most.

issues.title: Cab-Over Bunk Water Leaks
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~26%) — cab-over seams and front cap leak as sealant fails, causing soft floors.

issues.title: Chassis
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~22%) — the cutaway chassis (Ford E-series/Transit or Chevy) sees brake, suspension, and drivetrain wear under the heavy house.

issues.title: Slide-Outs
issues.blurb: Often reported (~20%) — slide rooms develop motor, gear, and seal problems that stick or leak.

issues.title: House Systems
issues.blurb: Reported (~16%) — water pump, converter, fridge, and propane appliances act up.

issues.title: Tire Age
issues.blurb: Reported (~10%) — rear tires age out before the tread wears and risk blowouts.

issues.title: Roof Sealant
issues.blurb: Reported (~6%) — roof and vent sealant cracks with age and lets water into the ceiling.
